title: Diogenes Laertius: Life of Anaximander
description: From Diogenes Laertius, Lives and Opinions of Eminent Philosophers — our main source for the lives of ancient philosophers.
introduction: From Diogenes Laertius, Lives and Opinions of Eminent Philosophers — our main source for the lives of ancient philosophers.

translation.text: Anaximander said that the apeiron — the boundless — is the first principle. He was the first to draw a map of the inhabited world. He believed humanity arose from creatures of the sea.
